reasoning: The informal statement is "if |G| = 462 then G is not simple". The Lean signature takes an arbitrary group G with a Fintype instance, assumes card G = 462, and concludes ¬ IsSimpleGroup G. The Fintype assumption is needed only to make `card G` well-typed, and card G = 462 already implies finiteness, so this is not an added constraint. Mathlib's `IsSimpleGroup` is defined as a `Nontrivial` group whose only normal subgroups are ⊥ and ⊤, exactly matching the standard definition of a simple group. The quantification over G is universal (via the type variable), matching the "for every group of order 462" reading of the informal claim. Hypotheses, conclusion, and types all match.
